title: Pleopeltis distans
taxonomy: Pleopeltis distans is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns. This species falls under the genus Pleopeltis, a group of epiphytic and terrestrial ferns. The species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pleopeltoideae. This classification is based on morphological and molecular studies of the plant. Pleopeltis distans is also known to be a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a large number of fern families.

benefits: Pleopeltis distans, also known as the brittle polypody, is a species of fern that has been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine in some parts of the world to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. side_effects: Pleopeltis distans is known to cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and dermatitis in some individuals who come into contact with its sap. This plant contains various chemical compounds, including saponins and glycosides, which can be toxic if ingested. intro: Pleopeltis distans, commonly known as the common polypody or distant polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis distans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support traditional medicine practices and promote overall well-being.
